ogTitle: Junction Hill vs Junee Reefs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Junction Hill and Junee Reefs.
heading: Junction Hill vs Junee Reefs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Junction Hill, New South Wales and Junee Reefs, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Junction Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Junee Reefs (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Junction Hill or Junee Reefs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Junction Hill has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Junee Reefs scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Junction Hill scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Junee Reefs scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Junction Hill or Junee Reefs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Junction Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
